items.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has made several major announcements in early 2026. Most significantly, on April 6, 2026, Anthropic expanded its partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute, with run-rate revenue reportedly surpassing $30 billion and over 1,000 business customers spending more than $1M annually. The company also announced a $50 billion investment in American AI infrastructure and is expanding global operations to India with a planned Bengaluru office. On the

items.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.6 - Anthropic
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had an active start to 2026 with several major model releases. Claude Opus 4.6 launched on February 5, 2026, featuring a 1-million-token context window, significantly improved coding and financial analysis capabilities, and an industry-leading 14.5-hour task completion time horizon. Claude Sonnet 4.6 followed on February 17, 2026, offering upgraded skills across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, and design — also with a 1M token context window in beta. T

items.title: Claude Code Releases - GitHub
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. On the model side, Claude Sonnet 4.6 launched as the latest balanced model with improved agentic search performance, extended thinking support, and a 1M token context window (beta). Claude Haiku 3 is being deprecated with retirement scheduled for April 19, 2026, with migration to Claude Haiku 4.5 recommended. The max_tokens cap was raised to 300k on the Message Batches API for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6, and the 1M token c

items.title: Anthropic Struck a Deal with Google and Broadcom for Gigawatt-Scale AI Capacity
items.author: incrypted.com
items.snippet:
Anthropic (@AnthropicAI) has been active with several major announcements in early April 2026. On April 7, 2026, Anthropic launched Project Glasswing in partnership with AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, the Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks, deploying Mythos Preview — a cybersecurity-focused AI capability — for finding and fixing flaws in critical infrastructure and open-source software across more than 40 participating organizations.

O
